hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century Ontario Cottage House-form building that reflects early workers’ housing within the District. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s, which included a large concentration of workers’ housing. Constructed as a pair, the properties retain their original asymmetrical composition including side gable with pointed arch window above a pair of windows. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 258. 50 Lippincott Street N/A 259. 52 Lippincott Street N/A 141 DCH | FO :D FO YTIC 5202 3 Nassau Street 3 NASSAU STREET

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, row, ontario cottage typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 3, 5 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a pair, the properties retain their original asymmetrical composition including side gable with pointed arch window above a pair of windows. 50 Lippincott Street N/A 259.

facade_description: Contemporary mid-rise apartment building (Kensington Lofts) in buff-beige brick, replacing the original pre-1889 building form on this lot. The ground level has a recessed glazed lobby entrance marked '160 Baldwin' with a dark grey surround, flanked by large aluminum-framed windows. Upper storeys feature modern window bands and glass-block panels in a symmetrical arrangement. A flat roof terminates in a clean parapet with no traditional cornice. Despite the HCD contributing designation, the current structure is entirely contemporary infill with no surviving historic fabric visible from the street.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a pair, the properties retain their original asymmetrical composition including side gable with pointed arch window above a pair of windows. 50 Lippincott Street N/A 259.
